lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Come to visit my garden then, have you, human?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is your garden?

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: No no, not quite. But I do look after it. It's the palace garden, you see. I keep it tidy, keep it living.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: It's hard to grow much on these hard rocks deep beneath these mountains, you know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can imagine. Where do you get all your food from? Or even the soil for this garden?

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: We can grow a little food underground, but most of it comes from the extensive steamcart network that runs beneath the earth.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: In the Era of Kings, many centuries ago, it was harder of course. Our steam technology was not developed as far yet and getting fresh supplies of food was always a problem.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Oh, I'm not boring you, am I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not at all! Please, go on!

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Oh yes, as I was saying, it was difficult to make a decent living here. I'm afraid this garden was rather neglected during that time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why did you dwarves stay so deep under ground, then? Why not simply go above ground?

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: What, with the wars raging up there?

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: We dwarves went underground a long time ago, see. For a while the city of Keldagrim was completely cut off from the rest of Gielinor simply waiting out the wars of the humans and the gods.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: This made it hard, though, to determine when the wars above the ground were over. Perhaps Keldagrim had remained hidden for far too long.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Only when King Alvis heroically defended the city against an invasion of trolls did we dare to venture out and see how much the world had changed.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Of course, after that...

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Ah well, enough history lessons I should say. I must really get back to my gardening.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the info!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A little, yes. I don't really know what all this has to do with anything.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: I suppose it doesn't, really, it's just the mumblings of an old dwarf.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not really, no.

lines.speaker: Rind the gardener
lines.text: Oh, alright then.
